definition: The contract for a deck's second-window PRESENTER VIEW — a separate window/pane showing the current slide, the next slide, the speaker notes, and a timer — kept in sync with the audience display on the SAME device. State (current slide-address, next, notes ref, timer start) is published over a BroadcastChannel; a `window.open` + `postMessage` handshake lets a late-joining or reopened presenter window request a full state snapshot (catch-up), and a heartbeat with reconnect keeps the two windows live across a closed/reopened channel. The defining invariant: the presenter window MUST NOT render the audience's View Transitions / build animations — it shows the resolved current+next state directly, never the in-between motion.
usage: Homed in webrealtime (the cross-window channel + handshake) and webportals (the second window surface); composes slide-addressing (the synced position), speaker-notes (the notes the presenter view renders), and the Advanceable-Sequence intent. Same-DEVICE cross-window — distinct from the cross-device remote-control command set (#1194), which mirrors across machines over a transport. The conformance footgun baked here: a late joiner or reconnect must reach the same state via the snapshot handshake, not by replaying every intervening command (no transition storm; instant-jump restore).
